text: Plan bundles three separate bets (workflow engine, routing policy, methodology templates) but only validates with N=1 manual evidence
Markdown pipeline spec is a DSL pretending to be markdown — executing it directly is a time bomb
Trust model covers confidentiality but not authority, tool egress, data-source sensitivity, or output exposure
Internal pipeline + openrouter phases contradicts the trust rules (internal only allows trusted + semi-trusted)
Roadmap order is wrong — pipeline IR, structured results, cancellation, human gates should come before routing
Templates should not be mutable runtime state in Munin — need versioning and reproducibility first
Internal inconsistencies: storage paths, runtime-hint vs registry IDs, conditions referencing undefined structured fields
Missing operational fundamentals: pipeline timeout, cancellation, resume, priority/preemption, idempotency
Fan-out in single-worker system is just queueing, not parallelism — plan should state this plainly
Heimdall pipeline card breaks existing architecture boundary (health-only, not service UI)
No success criteria defined — steps but no proof obligation
Steps 6-8 (OpenRouter, eval routing, Mac Studio) should be deferred from the software roadmap
Phase-level sensitivity downgrade is a confidentiality footgun — sensitivity must be monotonic (at least max of all inputs)
Step 2 (pipeline IR) must explicitly forbid conditions until Step 3 (structured results) ships
Authority field risks policy sprawl without typed side-effect contract and gate/resume/idempotency semantics
